summary: Backend-only testing for iteration 4 (cancel_reason on order status update). Discovered 2 critical issues: (1) OrderStatusPatch Pydantic model is missing the `cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None` field, so PATCH /api/orders/{id}/status with status='cancelled' crashes with HTTP 500 (AttributeError on body.cancel_reason). (2) The public preview URL (https://gratinalli-spets.preview.emergentagent.com) is returning 404 page not found for ALL /api/* routes (Cloudflare/ingress problem) while the same backend serves traffic correctly on localhost:8001. Tests were retargeted to localhost so the cancel_reason logic could be validated against the same FastAPI process; 10/12 tests pass, 2 fail with the exact same AttributeError shown in backend logs.

critical.issue: Returns HTTP 500 whenever status='cancelled' (with or without cancel_reason in body). Root cause: server.py L203 OrderStatusPatch model does NOT declare `cancel_reason`, but the handler at L538 references `body.cancel_reason`. Pydantic v2 strips unknown fields by default, so the attribute does not exist on the instance and `AttributeError: 'OrderStatusPatch' object has no attribute 'cancel_reason'` is raised. The customer cancel flow described in the requirements is completely non-functional.

https://gratinalli-spets.preview.emergentagent.com/api/* consistently returns Cloudflare '404 page not found' (content-length: 19, server: cloudflare, cf-ray header present) even though backend supervisor shows RUNNING and http://localhost:8001/api/products returns 200 with 38 products. One sporadic success was observed at ~18:15Z then all calls 404 from ~18:21Z onward including after supervisor restart. The K8s ingress / Cloudflare worker is no longer routing /api/* to port 8001. End-users (and frontend) cannot reach the backend through the preview domain.

minor.issue: Even after the model is fixed, the handler only sets `cancel_reason` when both status=='cancelled' AND cancel_reason is truthy. That matches the stated behavior, but be aware: if the FE sends an empty string for cancel_reason it will be silently dropped (no validation error).

action_items: FIX (1 line) in /app/backend/server.py around L203: add `cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None` to the OrderStatusPatch model. Without this, the entire 'Cancelar pedido' feature returns 500.

Investigate why https://gratinalli-spets.preview.emergentagent.com/api/* returns 404 page not found from Cloudflare while localhost:8001 works. This likely also explains the stale-bundle / 'old UI in preview' symptom the user reported. Check K8s ingress, the /api -> 8001 rewrite, and Cloudflare worker config. Until this is fixed, no end-to-end testing (frontend or external) of this app is possible.
After fixing OrderStatusPatch model and the ingress, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4_cancel_reason.py to confirm all 12 tests pass against the public URL.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L203 OrderStatusPatch is missing cancel_reason field while the handler at L538 reads body.cancel_reason – classic 'model/handler drift'. Suggest adding `cancel_reason: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, max_length=200)` to validate and constrain.
server.py L535-547 update_order_status does not validate state transitions (e.g., you can move 'delivered' -> 'received'). Not required by this iteration but worth noting.
server.py is 913 lines – exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting auth, orders, settings, delivery into separate routers.

The handler returns find_one() result directly; while _id is excluded by projection, no 404 happens if the doc was just updated but the find returns None due to a race – low risk but worth a defensive check.

success_rate.backend: 83% (10/12 tests; 2 failures are the cancel_reason bug)

test_credentials: admin@gratinalli.com / admin123 (works on localhost; cannot reach via public URL right now)
seed_data_creation: Created a fresh TEST_it4_* customer with phone and address (CEP 31130-600) and several test orders during the run. No special teardown needed (data is prefixed TEST_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4_cancel_reason.py auto-falls-back to http://localhost:8001 when the public URL returns non-200 on /api/products. After the main agent (a) adds `cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None` to OrderStatusPatch and (b) fixes the Cloudflare/ingress 404 issue, simply rerun: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://gratinalli-spets.preview.emergentagent.com python3 -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4_cancel_reason.py -v . All 12 tests should pass and the test will automatically use the public URL once it returns 200. Iteration 3 deltas (/auth/google, profile_completed, 30d cookie) and product/promotion/delivery regressions are already covered by test_iteration3_deltas.py and re-validated here.
rca of the issue: PATCH /api/orders/{id}/status with status='cancelled' -> 500. Reproduce: POST /auth/login as admin, POST /orders as a customer, then PATCH /api/orders/{id}/status {"status":"cancelled","cancel_reason":"Pagamento não confirmado"}. Backend log shows: File "/app/backend/server.py", line 538, in update_order_status: if body.status == "cancelled" and body.cancel_reason: AttributeError: 'OrderStatusPatch' object has no attribute 'cancel_reason'. Cause: Pydantic v2 model OrderStatusPatch (server.py L203) only declares `status` so unknown fields posted by the FE are dropped; accessing body.cancel_reason therefore raises. Mitigation: add `cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None` to OrderStatusPatch. After that the handler (which already gates persistence on `body.status == 'cancelled' and body.cancel_reason`) will work as designed: cancel_reason is saved only when status==cancelled and reason is non-empty, and is ignored for other statuses (no crash). The unrelated Cloudflare 404 problem must be investigated separately – probably ingress config or a worker route stripping /api/* on this preview domain.
